behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at watcher↔OS boundary (recursive watch behavior), not a single-function algorithm. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fake events can claim subdirectory events exist even when real watcher config misses them. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): call path-filter helper with nested path; catches bad path matching; misses watcher not emitting nested events. Current(System): run daemon against real temp directory tree, create file in nested subdir, assert one output artifact produced; catches non-recursive watch misconfig; misses very long-path edge cases. High(Workflow): full end-to-end multi-service pipeline; catches deployment wiring; unnecessary for this behavior. Rejected higher level: behavior text is only filesystem watch/processing and contains no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon as a real subprocess watching a temp root directory. Create `root/sub/a.txt`, wait for processing completion in output directory, and assert transformed output exists exactly once for that nested file.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is explicitly deduplication logic on `(path, time window)`, which is local decision logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): with fake clock, submit same path twice within window and assert second is dropped; catches window comparison/off-by-one bugs; misses wiring errors between watcher and deduper. Current(Unit): same as low plus boundary at exactly window expiry; catches inclusive/exclusive boundary defects. High(Integration): wire queue+deduper modules and publish duplicate requests; catches module contract mismatches; higher than needed for stated failure mode. Rejected higher levels: text says 'processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window' and does not require real FS events or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Test dedupe component in-process with a controllable clock: first request accepted, second same-path request inside window rejected, third after window accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when real OS emits duplicate event patterns; synthetic single-event mocks can always pass. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: exact duplicate/coalesced/changed event sequences are OS+watcher-library behavior. Candidate tests: Low(Integration): inject duplicate logical events into handler; catches missing idempotency in handler; misses watcher-level duplicate burst timing. Current(System): real watched dir, perform one write known to trigger multiple events, assert one processing output; catches duplicate-event amplification in production path. High(Workflow): multi-daemon pipeline; unnecessary because one daemon boundary is enough. Rejected higher level: no model-driven decision in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real filesystem watcher, perform a single file write that produces multiple OS events, and verify only one transformation/output record is created for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs in long-lived watcher reattachment after real directory deletion/recreation. REAL-INFRA: requires true OS directory lifecycle events and watcher backend behavior. Candidate tests: Low(Integration): call internal 'onDirectoryRecreated' hook and assert watcher re-init invoked; catches missed callback wiring; misses actual backend not re-subscribing. Current(System): start daemon, delete watched dir, recreate it, add new file, assert it is processed without restarting daemon; catches stale file descriptors/rewatch bugs. High(Workflow): external orchestrator restart/recovery chain; beyond behavior scope. Rejected higher level: behavior contains no AI/ML stage.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running as subprocess, remove watched directory, recreate same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed successfully without daemon restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering depends on timestamp metadata sourced from filesystem and near-simultaneous arrival races; pure mocks can hide timestamp resolution and event-order quirks. REAL-INFRA: creation-time semantics are OS/filesystem dependent. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): sort function over mocked timestamps; catches comparator bug; misses real metadata retrieval and race behavior. Current(System): create multiple files rapidly in real watched dir with controlled creation order, assert processing order follows observed creation timestamps; catches metadata-source and scheduling defects. High(Workflow): distributed multi-node ordering; not required by behavior text. Rejected higher level: no AI/ML output dependence stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real temp directory, create several files in quick succession, capture processing order, and assert it matches each file’s real creation timestamp order (with deterministic tie-break rule if needed).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a real timing/race condition between writer and watcher. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fake writes typically appear atomic and hide partial-write states. Candidate tests: Low(Integration): invoke 'onFileEvent' with mocked 'isComplete=false/true'; catches state-machine mistakes; misses actual partial-write detection failure. Current(System): write a large file in chunks with delays while daemon watches, assert processing starts only after final chunk/close and output reflects full content; catches premature processing races. High(Workflow): multi-agent pipeline around daemon; unnecessary for stated behavior. Rejected higher level: behavior is filesystem timing, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Using real filesystem I/O, stream-write a file in delayed chunks after creation event, verify daemon does not process during partial state, then verify exactly one successful processing after write completion.
